message:
perf: pre-convert attn + classifier x to f32 once per matmul

Same trick that paid off for experts: widen x from fp16 to f32 once
before every attention Q/K/V/O projection (and the classifier head)
and call a new matmul_f32_x_int4block kernel (f32 in, fp16 out). The
per-load fp16 widening in the inner MAC loop is gone — it runs once
per activation buffer instead of once per (N, block, T tile).

Node forward (T=61): 459 → 331 ms (-28%).
Browser bench:
  short  92 → 72 ms    (0.32× → 0.42× vs tjs)
  medium 237 → 176 ms  (0.16× → 0.22×)
  long   530 → 383 ms  (0.11× → 0.15×)

Cumulative since the ONNX pivot: 211/579/1341 → 72/176/383 ms
(2.9–3.5× faster across the board). 8/8 parity holds.
